best_practices: manage-tasks qgate-mechanical-checks keyword_drift findings are a standing consideration: when the mechanical-checks q-gate flags keyword drift between a description and its body, fold the correction into the work (taken_into_account) rather than suppressing it.
insights: Coverage instrumentation roughly triples runtime versus module-tests for this module, pushing past the host background-kill threshold. The command 'coverage plan-marshall' was killed twice with no output while module-tests of the same suite completed. Run coverage synchronously in the foreground with an extended Bash timeout of at least 600000ms rather than backgrounded. When coverage keeps getting killed, module-tests is the fallback verification signal.

build-maven and build-gradle each carry TWO independent path/glob classification tables that do not feed each other: classify_paths()/_CLASSIFY_PATTERNS (used by phase-4 aggregation) and classify_globs() (a separate glob-role table). A bug reported against the phase-4 unknown-bucket symptom is produced by _CLASSIFY_PATTERNS, not classify_globs(), even when a request names classify_globs() explicitly - root-cause each report against the correct table before scoping a fix.
key_dependencies_reasoning: Marketplace bundle - no compile-scope code dependencies; pure markdown skills + Python scripts using only stdlib

skills.build_systems.description: Build system abstraction over Maven, Gradle, npm, and pyproject: unified compile/test/verify/coverage command resolution plus per-system output parsing (JaCoCo, Cobertura, TAP).

skills.manage_architecture.description: Architecture analysis & enrichment manager - source of truth for module metadata, dependency graph, and skills_by_profile resolution. Consumed by phase-3-outline and phase-4-plan for placement decisions.

skills.phase_workflow.description: Six-phase planning workflow: phase-1-init through phase-6-finalize. Drives the end-to-end plan lifecycle (init → refine → outline → plan → execute → finalize) with strict boundary contracts.

skills.tools_script_executor.description: Generates and operates the .plan/execute-script.py proxy that resolves marketplace script notations (bundle:skill:script) to filesystem paths via embedded mappings.

purpose_reasoning: Plugin bundle exposing skills/agents/commands; no runtime entrypoint but is consumed by every other bundle and by Claude Code as a plugin
responsibility: Core orchestration bundle providing the complete 5-phase development workflow (init, refine, outline, plan, execute, finalize), task planning, git/PR workflows, Sonar integration, and build automation across Maven/Gradle/npm/pyproject. Hosts foundational infrastructure (script executor, manage-config, manage-architecture, manage-lessons, permissions, logging) that every other bundle consumes.

skills_by_profile_reasoning: plan-marshall-plugin-dev: marshall-plugin build_system; bundle authors marketplace skills + scripts (optionals apply for script-heavy work); Batch: marshall-plugin build_system detected; all marketplace bundles host skills/scripts authored under plugin-architecture standards
tips: generate_executor.discover_scripts propagates the marketplace root to its scan-marketplace-inventory subprocess ONLY via the PM_MARKETPLACE_ROOT env-var anchor, never the --marketplace-root flag. Consumer-chain tests must anchor via that env var with use_flag false, or version-aware discovery silently degrades to the version-unaware glob fallback.

In fnmatch-based glob classify tables (build-maven/build-gradle classify_globs and _CLASSIFY_PATTERNS), a double-star prefix does not also match a bare repo-root file: the pattern star-star-slash-star-dot-sh still misses a root-level foo.sh because a single star in fnmatch spans the path separator too. Use a plain star-dot-ext row with no double-star prefix as the minimum pattern that additionally covers the repo root.

Coverage scoping via build-pyproject is bundle-level only, unlike module-tests which can target a sub-module. A bundle-name coverage run works but a sub-module such as workflow-integration-git cannot be coverage-scoped. Whole-tree and single-bundle coverage routinely exceed the 600s Bash ceiling, get backgrounded, and are harness-killed with zero bytes, the externally-killed class mitigated by PR 912. Do not blind-retry a killed coverage job. Fall back to a bundle-scoped foreground coverage run that fits under the ceiling, and treat CI whole-tree coverage as the authority.
